title: Vanilla Horizons
description: A modpack that expands Minecraft with realism, atmosphere, ui and subtle improvements. | 1.21 | fabric
body:
## What is the focus of the modpack?

- **Performance**
- **Quality of Life & Interface**
- **Visuals**![Snowy cherry grove](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/cbd32dba6503581a5527ec0cd195c3238d970100.png)
- **Achievements**![Advancements](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e68d82720153c23044a12c6ceb8f85b876c8dfbc.png)
- **Redstone**![New redstone](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ea8f5f21754d0af13934a9be1d5eca19c3bdf8f4.png)
- **Exploration**![New structures](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/46e8993f1fe18025cedf0add991576937bd5181f.png)
- **Survival**![Survival](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/99b33b473b67982afa8236dc695b09967e60c241.png)

## About the Modpack

Vanilla Horizons is a vanilla-plus modpack that expands the vanilla Minecraft experience.
This modpack includes performance improvements, immersive visuals, more visual effects, 3D textures, quality‑of‑life tweaks, more achievements, more redstone, exploration content and survival mechanics.

### How to use the modpack in singleplayer and multiplayer:
The modpack is designed for singleplayer and multiplayer via Essential. If you are playing with Essential make sure the host and all clients are using the same modpack version.  If you are on a dedicated server the server and the clients also need to have the same modpack version but the modpack is not tested on a dedicated server because I do not own one.

## Which version of the modpack do I use? The Lite version or the normal version? _README_
### Default modpack settings
- Normal version: required 24gb+ ram with minimal 18gb ram allocated to the modpack, recommended is to allocate 20gb+ of ram to the modpack.
- Lite version: recommended 8gb+ ram with minimal 3,7gb ram allocated to the modpack, recommended is to allocate 5gb+ of ram to the modpack.
 ### Which version do I use
- I have a decent pc. > Normal version.
- I have a low-end gpu or integrated graphics. > Lite version.
- I have a low-end cpu. > Lite version.
- I have a low-end pc. > Lite version.
- I have _16gb ram_. > Lite version.
- I have _8gb ram_. > Lite version. Allocate 3,7 gb ram to the modpack.
- **_For the normal and Lite version follow these steps:_**
1. Go to options > Stutterfix.
2. Set worker threads count to the max.
3. Leave rest as is.


<details>
<summary>Important Keybinds & Features</summary>

## Important Keybinds & Features

**JEI Item & Recipe Guide**
You can find almost every recipe in the game using JEI.
Open your inventory and use the menu on the right to browse items, check recipes, and discover content not listed here.

**New survival Mechanics: Temperature & Thirst**
- Craft a canteen to drink water.
- Make wool armor to stay warm or leaf armor to stay cool.
- Winter times can get very cold.

**Paragliders**

Paragliders make exploration much easier.
- When equipped, you can gain altitude by flying over hot air, such as a fire lit with flint and steel.

**Bundles**

Bundles do not appear in JEI, but you can craft them:
- 1 leather + 1 string above the leather

**Crawl**

Press X to crawl. Great for fitting into 1 block spaces.

**Zoom**

Press C to zoom in and see faraway.

**Screenshot Manager**

Screenshots taken with F4 can be viewed in‑game.
In both the main menu and pause menu, you’ll find in the bottom of your screen:
- A Screenshot Viewer
- A Notebook for writing down tasks

**Seasons**

Seasons are now in Minecraft.
- Winter is very cold in cold biomes.
- Summers are very hot in warm biomes.
- Neutral biomes are in between.
- Some crops do not grow during winter. Hover over seeds or crops in your inventory to see their seasonal growth info.

**Enchanted Book Descriptions**

Unsure what an enchanted book does?
Most books can be right‑clicked to read a description.

**Wiki**
- G: open the wiki for the block you’re looking at.
- H: open the wiki search menu for general queries.

**Chunk Loaders**

You can craft chunk loaders to keep areas active when yourn't nearby.
- Press V to view and manage them.

**Waypoints and Minimap**
- N: add a waypoint.
- B: create a quick waypoint (perfect when running from danger).
- M: enlarge the minimap.

**Right‑Click Harvest**

Right‑click crops to harvest and replant them instantly.


<details>
<summary>All important keybinds:</summary>

- X - Crawl
- Z - Advancements
- C - Zoom 
- V - Chunk loaders
- R - Sort inventory
- G - Open wiki
- H - Search in wiki
- ] - Orthographic camera
- = - Zoom in
- Zoom out is -
- ` - Emotes
- M - Big minimap
- B - Open waypoint screen
- N - Quick waypoint
- [ - Change zoom minimap
- L - Toggle shaders
- F4 - Screenshot
- F9 - Cinematic camera
- F11 - Fullscreen
- F2 - Perspective
- F10 - Detach camera
- Left alt - Axiom menu (creative only)

</details>




</details>
